commit.message:
fix: show allocation/exchange as single internal transfer row in finance journal

Previously, allocation transactions appeared as two confusing rows (+5000 and -5000).
Now: hide the IN side, show only OUT as "Внутрішній переказ" with transfer icon,
no +/- sign, and zero impact on running balance. Same treatment for exchanges.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(nav): prevent scroll chain from left nav bleeding into right panel

Added overscroll-behavior: contain to nav (desktop sticky + mobile fixed).
Without it, scroll events at the nav boundary propagated to the window,
moving the page and the right panel, and desynchronising sticky scroll
state — causing the nav to get stuck until the right panel was reset.

WL-085

Add IYCF indicator module and Streamlit dashboard GUI

New files:
- code/11_iycf_indicators.py: Computes 8 UNICEF/WHO 2021 IYCF indicators
  (MDD, MMF, MAD, EFF, ZVF, SwB, UFC) from food events + NAR/MAR from

  nutrients show >50% inadequacy (vitamin A worst at 97.2%).

commit.message:
fix: remove nested button hydration errors in FeedPageClient and LeaguesClient

InfoTooltip (which uses TooltipTrigger) was nested inside native <button>
elements in two places, causing React hydration errors due to invalid HTML.

- FeedPageClient: restructured filter tab buttons to use TooltipTrigger
  asChild wrapping the button directly, instead of InfoTooltip inside button
- LeaguesClient: restructured tier expand buttons similarly — button is now
  the TooltipTrigger (asChild), preserving the tier explanation tooltip

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Book hover lift, slide-down on modal close, nav/button polish

- Hover: use React state (isHovered) instead of direct DOM manipulation
  so CSS transitions work reliably against animation fill-mode; lift is 9px
  with enhanced drop shadow so it's clearly visible when sweeping cursor
- Modal close: book slides DOWN onto shelf (translateY -14px→0, 0.45s)
  instead of sliding up; uses prevIsPulledRef to detect the true→false
  transition; hasMounted flag prevents initial slideUp from re-triggering
- Back panel overflow:hidden prevents any scrollbar blip during animations
- Add Book button: top -31px (exactly 1px above wood frame border)
- Nav sticky bar: background opacity reduced to 0.6, blur increased to 8px

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: hero/logo upload uses unique filename per upload

Each upload now gets a timestamp-based filename (hero-1710012345.jpg)
instead of overwriting hero.jpg. This avoids CDN cache issues and
removes the need for upsert (plain INSERT = simplest RLS path).
Also shows alert on upload failure for immediate user feedback.

commit.message:
fix: Clio import dies at ~136/266 docs — BackgroundTask killed on Vercel

BackgroundTasks get terminated when the Vercel function returns its response.
For large imports (266 docs), the background task runs ~360s before being
silently killed, leaving the import stuck mid-way.

Fix: Add /run-import StreamingResponse endpoint that keeps the Vercel function
alive via heartbeats. Frontend fire-and-forgets this call after create-from-clio.
Import is naturally resumable via existing duplicate detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Props Pass 1 used game pick framework — new buildPass1PropsMessage for props

- Pass 1 nudge messages referenced "research briefing" (Flash doesn't run for props)
- Duplicate KEY FACTOR output steps removed from NBA/NFL/NHL props constitutions

commit.message:
Add phonetic Z↔S normalization with prefix matching for bairro names

Handles cases like Alvorada "FORMOZA" → phonetic "FORMOSA" → prefix
match to polygon "FORMOSA / MARIA REGINA" (3473 records). Brazilian
Portuguese commonly interchanges Z/S spelling (Formoza/Formosa,
Tereza/Teresa, Rezende/Resende). Only matches when phonetically
corrected name is a unique prefix of a polygon name.

commit.message:
feat: v2.1.0 - scoring rebalance (55/30/15) with coherence gate

Weight distribution redesigned based on real-world AI citation analysis:
- Content Substance: ~55% (topic coherence, original data, depth, facts)
- Content Organization: ~30% (entity, linking, freshness, schema)
- Technical Plumbing: ~15% (llms.txt, robots, RSS, canonical)

Added coherence gate: sites with topic coherence < 6/10 are score-capped
regardless of technical perfection. Prevents technically perfect but
content-scattered sites from receiving inflated scores.

Key demotions: Schema.org 8%->3%, llms.txt 8%->2%, Clean HTML 8%->2%
Key holds: Topic Coherence 14%, Original Data 10%

Also fixes v2.0 bugs:
- Added missing topic_coherence + content_depth to opportunity templates
- Added missing fix generators for both new criteria
- Fixed per-page scorer weights to match site-level tiers
- Fixed stale "26 criteria" references -> "28 criteria"

Updated README with grouped-by-tier criteria tables and per-page
scoring criteria breakdown.

566 tests passing.

Wrap mcp-gw with baked-in gateway URL

ZeroClaw sanitizes the environment for spawned subprocesses, so env vars
set on the systemd service don't reach mcp-gw. Use a wrapper script that
exports MCP_GATEWAY_URL before exec'ing the real binary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Compress video via Cloudinary before passing to Apify

ffmpeg-wasm is browser-only; budget quality from Popcorn still exceeds
5MB. Cloudinary upload with 500kbps bitrate cap brings a 15s clip to
~937KB — well under Apify's 5MB limit. No binary needed, free tier.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(pr-poller): remove unsupported 'conclusion' field from gh pr checks query

Older versions of the gh CLI do not expose 'conclusion' as a JSON field
for `gh pr checks --json`, causing the command to fail with
"Unknown JSON field: conclusion" and blocking all pipeline checks.

Remove 'conclusion' from the --json query so the command works on all
gh versions. The 'state' field returned by gh pr checks already reflects
the effective check state (incorporating conclusion when available), so
pipeline status analysis remains correct. Made GhCheckRun.conclusion
optional to match real-world behaviour across gh versions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

related session state, function parameters, and history fields to
eliminate Streamlit widget re-mounting issues.

commit.message:
Fix bairro detail panel showing different count than heatmap marker

Two bugs fixed:
1. location_stats endpoint was missing ultimos_meses parameter, so the
   detail panel queried all-time data while the heatmap filtered to last
   12 months. Added ultimos_meses to both Crime and CrimeStaging queries.
2. Detail panel replaced heatmap count with stats.total from location_stats,
   but location_stats uses exact bairro name matching while heatmap uses
   fuzzy merge. Now the detail panel keeps the heatmap count as authoritative.

Frontend forwards ultimos_meses in all 5 fetchLocationStats call sites.

commit.message:
fix: prevent SSH input freezing and keystroke drops

Cap backend event drain to 128 events per iteration to prevent terminal
output from starving keystroke processing. Increase broadcast channel
buffer (256→4096) and command channel buffer (256→1024) to prevent
silent event loss and send-side blocking under heavy terminal output.

commit.message:
docs(protocol): reviews must be self-contained for cold agent handoff

Any changes_requested review must include enough context for an agent
with no prior knowledge to read only the PR thread and implement the fix.

This ensures abandoned PRs can be picked up by any agent without
losing context or requiring human re-explanation.

feat(install): add --interval and --cron scheduling options

--interval sets a custom systemd OnUnitActiveSec duration (e.g. 30min, 1h).
--cron installs a real crontab entry using the script path as unique identifier
for reliable removal — no inline comment, fully portable across cron implementations.
uninstall auto-detects and removes both systemd and crontab installations.

commit.message:
CR-17: Unique CR numbers for PR comments + production review links

PR comment CRs now auto-create a GitHub Issue for unique tracking.
Each change gets its own CR number (CR-18, CR-19, etc.) instead of
reusing the PR number. Completion notifier cross-posts back to the
source PR and always links to the production review URL.

Changes:
- cr-pr-comment.yml: create tracking issue per comment, acknowledge
  with CR number on both PR and issue
- cr-completion-notifier.yml: include CR number in headings, cross-post
  to source PR, use production URL for review links

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
